message:
fix(perf): grant issues:write so reusable notify workflow can start (#6168)

All three perf workflows (react-commits, bundle-size, ttfi) declared
`permissions: read-all` at the top level, but their `notify` jobs call
the reusable `_perf-regression-issue.yml` workflow which needs
`issues: write` to file/update the auto-regression issue.

GitHub validates reusable-workflow permissions at workflow startup,
before `if:` conditions are evaluated. Because the caller had no
`issues: write` to delegate, all three runs hit `startup_failure`
with zero jobs and no logs the moment they were dispatched.

Fix: replace `read-all` with an explicit least-privilege block that
grants `contents: read`, `actions: read`, and `issues: write` on each
caller. This matches what the reusable workflow already declares.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -16,7 +16,10 @@ on:
     - cron: '15 */2 * * *'
   workflow_dispatch: {}
 
-permissions: read-all
+permissions:
+  contents: read
+  actions: read
+  issues: write
 
 concurrency:
   group: perf-bundle-size

@@ -15,7 +15,10 @@ on:
     - cron: '15 */2 * * *'
   workflow_dispatch: {}
 
-permissions: read-all
+permissions:
+  contents: read
+  actions: read
+  issues: write
 
 concurrency:
   group: perf-react-commits

@@ -14,8 +14,14 @@ on:
     # `[perf-regression] ttfi-all-cards` issue via the reusable workflow.
     - cron: '15 */2 * * *'
 
-# Least-privilege: read-only by default; jobs declare write scopes individually
-permissions: read-all
+# Least-privilege: read + issues:write. issues:write is required at the
+# workflow level because the `notify` job calls the reusable
+# `_perf-regression-issue.yml` workflow, and reusable workflows cannot
+# receive more permissions than their caller.
+permissions:
+  contents: read
+  actions: read
+  issues: write
 
 env:
   CI: true
